zh.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbell於1310年出生在英國蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾度巴頓。她於1330年之前與Malcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chief在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1340年在蘇格蘭艾丁波享年30歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾易納。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothea
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a post-classical Greek name, from dōron ‘gift’ + theos ‘god’ (the same elements as in Theodora , but in reverse order). The masculine form Dorotheus was borne by several early Christian saints, the feminine by only two minor ones, but only the girl's name has survived. In modern use in the English-speaking world it represents either a 19th-century Latinization of Dorothy or a learned reborrowing.

nameMeanings.name: Marjorie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual modern spelling of Margery . It seems to have arisen as the result of folk etymological association of the name with that of the herb marjoram ( compare Rosemary ). This word is of uncertain origin; its Middle English and Old French form was majorane, without the first -r-.

variants.name: Marjory
meanings: The usual modern spelling of Margery . It seems to have arisen as the result of folk etymological association of the name with that of the herb marjoram ( compare Rosemary ). This word is of uncertain origin; its Middle English and Old French form was majorane, without the first -r-.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbell was born in 1310, in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Malcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chief before 1330, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1340, in Edinburgh, Scotland, at the age of 30, and was buried in Iona, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbell est née en 1310, à Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Malcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chief avant 1330, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1340, à Edinburgh, Écosse, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterrée à Iona, Argyll, Écosse.

Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb 1340 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Edinburgh, Scotland und wurde in Iona, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbellは1310年にイギリススコットランドダンバートンシュアイアダンバートンで生まれた。1330年以前にスコットランドでMalcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chiefと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1340年にスコットランドエディンブルグで30歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルアイオナにて埋葬された。

ko.Stornoway, Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Sc: 영국 스코틀랜드 Comhairle nan Eilean Siar 스톤웨이
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbell(은)는 1310년 영국 스코틀랜드 던바토실르 드바턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1330년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Malcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chief(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1340년 스컷랜드 에딘버그h에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 이오나에 묻혔다.

ru.Stornoway, Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Sc: сторноуеи, Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Lady Dorothea Marjorie Campbell родилась в 1310 г. в местности думбартон, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Malcolm MacLeod 3rd Clan Chief перед 1330 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1340 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия в возрасте 30 лет и была похоронена в местности иона, аргилл, Шотландия.
